content.question: Whether Hungarian church-school expansion since 2010 is primarily a fiscal/capacity response to state withdrawal (incidental segregation), a deliberate targeting of disadvantaged populations (intentional cream-skimming), or an unintended consequence of decentralized FBO funding incentives—and whether this distinction implies different policy interventions.
content.reduction_path: Comparative case studies of 6–8 FBO-operated rural schools and matched state-operated control schools, including interviews with leadership, enrollment-process documentation, and outcome data (achievement, retention, future enrollment destination) disaggregated by socioeconomic/ethnic subgroup to isolate selection versus peer-effect mechanisms.
